paper_title: Synthesis of 3D ordered macroporous TiO2-supported Au nanoparticle photocatalysts and their photocatalytic performances for the reduction of CO2 to methane

catalyst.source: Synthesis of 3D ordered macroporous TiO2-supported Au nanoparticle photocatalysts and their photocatalytic performances for the reduction of CO2 to methane
catalyst.context: Synthesis of 3D ordered macroporous TiO2-supported Au nanoparticle photocatalysts and their photocatalytic performances for the reduction of CO2 to methane

catalyst.source: This work presents the design and preparation of three-dimensionally ordered macroporous (3DOM) TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with enhanced visible-light-responsive properties for the application in carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) photoreduction.
catalyst.context: This work presents the design and preparation of three-dimensionally ordered macroporous (3DOM) TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with enhanced visible-light-responsive properties for the application in carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) photoreduction.The 3DOM TiO 2 support was prepared using a colloidal crystal template (CCT) method.A series of photocatalysts of TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with uniform sizes were successfully synthesized by the facile method of gas bubbling-assisted membrane reduction (GBMR).These photocatalysts were characterized by scanning electron microscopy (SEM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), X-ray diffraction (XRD), UV-vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy, Brunauer-Emmet-Teller (BET) analysis of nitrogen adsorption isotherm, photoluminescence spectra (PL), and the surface photovoltage (SPV) technique.All the catalysts possess well-defined 3DOM structures, which are highly ordered and interconnected with one another by small pore windows, and the Au nanoparticles are uniformly dispersed and supported on the inner wall of the uniform macropores.The slow light effect of the 3DOM structure can enhance absorption efficiency of solar irradiation.And the introduction of Au nanoparticles can effectively extend the spectral response from UV to visible region owing to Au surface plasmon resonance (SPR), and it is favorable for enhancing the separation of photoinduced electron-hole pairs.3DOM AuTiO 2 photocatalysts exhibit high catalytic activity for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O to CH 4 under visible illumination.

catalyst.source: And the introduction of Au nanoparticles can effectively extend the spectral response from UV to visible region owing to Au surface plasmon resonance (SPR), and it is favorable for enhancing the separation of photoinduced electron-hole pairs.
catalyst.context: This work presents the design and preparation of three-dimensionally ordered macroporous (3DOM) TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with enhanced visible-light-responsive properties for the application in carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) photoreduction.The 3DOM TiO 2 support was prepared using a colloidal crystal template (CCT) method.A series of photocatalysts of TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with uniform sizes were successfully synthesized by the facile method of gas bubbling-assisted membrane reduction (GBMR).These photocatalysts were characterized by scanning electron microscopy (SEM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), X-ray diffraction (XRD), UV-vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy, Brunauer-Emmet-Teller (BET) analysis of nitrogen adsorption isotherm, photoluminescence spectra (PL), and the surface photovoltage (SPV) technique.All the catalysts possess well-defined 3DOM structures, which are highly ordered and interconnected with one another by small pore windows, and the Au nanoparticles are uniformly dispersed and supported on the inner wall of the uniform macropores.The slow light effect of the 3DOM structure can enhance absorption efficiency of solar irradiation.And the introduction of Au nanoparticles can effectively extend the spectral response from UV to visible region owing to Au surface plasmon resonance (SPR), and it is favorable for enhancing the separation of photoinduced electron-hole pairs.3DOM AuTiO 2 photocatalysts exhibit high catalytic activity for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O to CH 4 under visible illumination.

catalyst.source: The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles (3DOM Au n TiO 2 , where the weight ratio of Au to TiO 2 is n100) were synthesized by the GBMR technique which is described in detail in our previous reports 171819.
catalyst.context: The poly(methyl methacrylate) (PMMA) colloidal crystaltemplating methods were used for the preparation of 3DOM titanium dioxide using tetrabutyl titanate as precursor solution.In a typical calcination, the temperature-rising rate was 1 C min -1 from room temperature to 600 C. The dried sample was calcined at 600 C for 5 h to remove the CCT at an air flow of 80 ml min -1 in a tube furnace.Finally, a 3DOM TiO 2 support was obtained 16. The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles (3DOM Au n TiO 2 , where the weight ratio of Au to TiO 2 is n100) were synthesized by the GBMR technique which is described in detail in our previous reports 171819. Powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns were recorded with a diffractometer (Shimadzu XRD 6000) using Cu K ( 0.15406 nm) radiation with a Nickel filter operating at 40 kV and 10 mA in the 2 range of 10-80 with a step of 0.02 0.2 s.The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface areas of the powders were measured by nitrogen adsorption in an Autosorb iQASiQwin Quantachrome Gas Sorption Analyzer (U.S.A).The UV-vis DRS experiments were performed in the range of 200-800 nm on a UV-vis spectrophotometer (Hitachi U-4100) and BaSO 4 was used as a reference.The TEM and HRTEM images were obtained by a JEOl JEM-2100 electron microscope with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.The SEM images were examined with scanning electron microscopy of FEI Quanta 200F at an accelerating voltage of 30 kV.X-ray photoelectron spectra (XPS) were recorded with a Perkin-Elmer PHI-1600 ESCA spectrometer using Mg Ka (h 1253.6eV) X-ray source.The photoluminescence (PL) spectra of the as-prepared samples were measured using a Hitachi F-4600 fluorescence spectrophotometer.The metal ions were determined by inductively coupled plasma atomic emission spectrometer.The surface photovoltage (SPV) measurement was performed with a lock-in based SPV measurement system at room temperature and in air atmosphere. The activity measurements for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O were carried out in a gas-closed circulation system 19.A Xe lamp (300 W) purchased from Perfect Light Co.Light with a 420 nm UV-cutoff filter was used as the visible-light source.The reaction products were analyzed by using an on-line gas chromatograph (GC-9560; HuaAiSePu Corp., China).The photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O was typically carried out at 298 K for 480 min in each run and samples were continually taken from the reaction cell at given intervals (40 min) for quantitative analysis based on the external standard by using a calibration curve.

catalyst.source: The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au with different contents were successfully synthesized by the GBMR method.
catalyst.context: In summary, the 3DOM TiO 2 support was prepared by the colloidal crystal template method using tetrabutyl titanate as precursor solution.The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au with different contents were successfully synthesized by the GBMR method.The structure of 3DOM AuTiO 2 catalysts is well defined and uniform, and the Au nanoparticles deposited on the inner wall of 3DOM TiO 2 are highly dispersed and uniform in size.The slow light effect of the 3DOM structure can enhance absorption efficiency of solar irradiation and the ordered macropore enhances the diffusion of reactant molecules.Moreover, the Au nanoparticles deposited on the TiO 2 surface can absorb more visible light and induce a stronger SPR effect on the Au NPs for the excitation of electrons to TiO 2 to reduce CO 2 into CH 4 .3DOM AuTiO 2 catalysts showed excellent performance for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O to produce CH 4 .Among the as-prepared samples, the 3DOM Au 8 TiO 2 catalyst presents the highest CH 4 production amount (23.10 mol g -1 ) during 480 min light irradiation.The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticle may be useful for fundamental research on metal-support synergetic effect and potential practical applications in the catalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O to produce CH 4 . Fig. 1.XRD patterns of 3DOM TiO2 and AuTiO2 catalysts prepared by the GBMR method.(a) TiO2; (b) Au0.5TiO2; (c) Au1TiO2; (d) Au2TiO2; (e) Au4TiO2; (f) Au8TiO2. Fig. 2. SEM images of 3DOM TiO2 and AuTiO2 catalysts prepared by the GBMR method.(A) TiO2; (B) Au0.5TiO2; (C) Au1TiO2; (D) Au2TiO2; (E) Au4TiO2; (F) Au8TiO2. Fig. 3. TEM (A), HRTEM (B), and size distribution of Au nanoparticles (C) of 3DOM Au4TiO2 catalysts. Fig. 4. Nitrogen adsorption-desorption isotherms of 3DOM TiO2 and AuTiO2 catalysts.(a) TiO2; (b) Au1TiO2; (c) Au4TiO2. Fig. 5. Ti 2p (A), O 1s (B), and Au 4f (C) XPS spectra of 3DOM TiO2 and AuTiO2 catalysts.(a) TiO2; (b) Au4TiO2. Fig. 6.UV-vis DRS of 3DOM TiO2 and AuTiO2 catalysts with different Au loadings.(a) P25; (b) TiO2; (c) Au0.5TiO2;(d) Au1TiO2; (e)Au2TiO2; (f) Au4TiO2; (g) Au8TiO2. Fig. 7.The SPV of 3DOM TiO2 and AuTiO2 catalysts in the UV region.(a) TiO2; (b) Au0.5TiO2; (c) Au1TiO2; (d) Au2TiO2; (e) Au4TiO2; (f) Au8TiO2. Fig. 8. Photoluminescence spectra of P25, 3DOM TiO2, and Au4TiO2 catalysts.(a) P25; (b) TiO2; (c) Au4TiO2. Fig. 9. CH4 production amounts of P25, 3DOM TiO2, and AuTiO2 catalysts.(a) P25; (b) TiO2; (c) Au0.5TiO2;(d) Au1TiO2; (e) Au2TiO2; (f) Au4TiO2; (g) Au8TiO2. J. Jiao et al. Catalysis Today xxx (2015) xxx-xxx 3 Average crystal parameters and crystal sizes of 3DOM TiO2 and AunTiO2 catalysts. a Determined by XRD using the Scherer equation with the intensity of the most prominent peak, (101).b Determined by TEM.c Surface area obtained by the BET method.d Determined by the ICP-AES technique.e Eg values obtained by Kubelka-Munk transformed reflectance spectra. The mechanism for photocatalytic reduction of CO2 with H2O to methane over 3DOM AuTiO2 catalysts.reduction proceeds through a series of elementary steps prior to generating CH 4 finally.At the same time, the holes of Au nanoparticles can oxidize H 2 O to O 2 and H . CO 2 -.The Please cite this article in press as: J. Jiao, et al., Synthesis of 3D ordered macroporous TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticle photocatalysts and their photocatalytic performances for the reduction of CO 2 to methane, Catal.Today (2015), http:dx.doi.org10.1016j.cattod.2015.01.030Scheme 1. J. Jiao et al. light source.source: 3DOM AuTiO 2 photocatalysts exhibit high catalytic activity for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O to CH 4 under visible illumination.
light source.context: This work presents the design and preparation of three-dimensionally ordered macroporous (3DOM) TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with enhanced visible-light-responsive properties for the application in carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) photoreduction.The 3DOM TiO 2 support was prepared using a colloidal crystal template (CCT) method.A series of photocatalysts of TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles with uniform sizes were successfully synthesized by the facile method of gas bubbling-assisted membrane reduction (GBMR).These photocatalysts were characterized by scanning electron microscopy (SEM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), X-ray diffraction (XRD), UV-vis diffuse reflectance spectroscopy, Brunauer-Emmet-Teller (BET) analysis of nitrogen adsorption isotherm, photoluminescence spectra (PL), and the surface photovoltage (SPV) technique.All the catalysts possess well-defined 3DOM structures, which are highly ordered and interconnected with one another by small pore windows, and the Au nanoparticles are uniformly dispersed and supported on the inner wall of the uniform macropores.The slow light effect of the 3DOM structure can enhance absorption efficiency of solar irradiation.And the introduction of Au nanoparticles can effectively extend the spectral response from UV to visible region owing to Au surface plasmon resonance (SPR), and it is favorable for enhancing the separation of photoinduced electron-hole pairs.3DOM AuTiO 2 photocatalysts exhibit high catalytic activity for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O to CH 4 under visible illumination.

light source.source: Light with a 420 nm UV-cutoff filter was used as the visible-light source.
light source.context: The poly(methyl methacrylate) (PMMA) colloidal crystaltemplating methods were used for the preparation of 3DOM titanium dioxide using tetrabutyl titanate as precursor solution.In a typical calcination, the temperature-rising rate was 1 C min -1 from room temperature to 600 C. The dried sample was calcined at 600 C for 5 h to remove the CCT at an air flow of 80 ml min -1 in a tube furnace.Finally, a 3DOM TiO 2 support was obtained 16. The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles (3DOM Au n TiO 2 , where the weight ratio of Au to TiO 2 is n100) were synthesized by the GBMR technique which is described in detail in our previous reports 171819. Powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns were recorded with a diffractometer (Shimadzu XRD 6000) using Cu K ( 0.15406 nm) radiation with a Nickel filter operating at 40 kV and 10 mA in the 2 range of 10-80 with a step of 0.02 0.2 s.The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface areas of the powders were measured by nitrogen adsorption in an Autosorb iQASiQwin Quantachrome Gas Sorption Analyzer (U.S.A).The UV-vis DRS experiments were performed in the range of 200-800 nm on a UV-vis spectrophotometer (Hitachi U-4100) and BaSO 4 was used as a reference.The TEM and HRTEM images were obtained by a JEOl JEM-2100 electron microscope with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.The SEM images were examined with scanning electron microscopy of FEI Quanta 200F at an accelerating voltage of 30 kV.X-ray photoelectron spectra (XPS) were recorded with a Perkin-Elmer PHI-1600 ESCA spectrometer using Mg Ka (h 1253.6eV) X-ray source.The photoluminescence (PL) spectra of the as-prepared samples were measured using a Hitachi F-4600 fluorescence spectrophotometer.The metal ions were determined by inductively coupled plasma atomic emission spectrometer.The surface photovoltage (SPV) measurement was performed with a lock-in based SPV measurement system at room temperature and in air atmosphere. The activity measurements for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O were carried out in a gas-closed circulation system 19.A Xe lamp (300 W) purchased from Perfect Light Co.Light with a 420 nm UV-cutoff filter was used as the visible-light source.The reaction products were analyzed by using an on-line gas chromatograph (GC-9560; HuaAiSePu Corp., China).The photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O was typically carried out at 298 K for 480 min in each run and samples were continually taken from the reaction cell at given intervals (40 min) for quantitative analysis based on the external standard by using a calibration curve.

lamp.source: A Xe lamp (300 W) purchased from Perfect Light Co.
lamp.context: The poly(methyl methacrylate) (PMMA) colloidal crystaltemplating methods were used for the preparation of 3DOM titanium dioxide using tetrabutyl titanate as precursor solution.In a typical calcination, the temperature-rising rate was 1 C min -1 from room temperature to 600 C. The dried sample was calcined at 600 C for 5 h to remove the CCT at an air flow of 80 ml min -1 in a tube furnace.Finally, a 3DOM TiO 2 support was obtained 16. The photocatalysts of 3DOM TiO 2 -supported Au nanoparticles (3DOM Au n TiO 2 , where the weight ratio of Au to TiO 2 is n100) were synthesized by the GBMR technique which is described in detail in our previous reports 171819. Powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns were recorded with a diffractometer (Shimadzu XRD 6000) using Cu K ( 0.15406 nm) radiation with a Nickel filter operating at 40 kV and 10 mA in the 2 range of 10-80 with a step of 0.02 0.2 s.The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface areas of the powders were measured by nitrogen adsorption in an Autosorb iQASiQwin Quantachrome Gas Sorption Analyzer (U.S.A).The UV-vis DRS experiments were performed in the range of 200-800 nm on a UV-vis spectrophotometer (Hitachi U-4100) and BaSO 4 was used as a reference.The TEM and HRTEM images were obtained by a JEOl JEM-2100 electron microscope with an accelerating voltage of 200 kV.The SEM images were examined with scanning electron microscopy of FEI Quanta 200F at an accelerating voltage of 30 kV.X-ray photoelectron spectra (XPS) were recorded with a Perkin-Elmer PHI-1600 ESCA spectrometer using Mg Ka (h 1253.6eV) X-ray source.The photoluminescence (PL) spectra of the as-prepared samples were measured using a Hitachi F-4600 fluorescence spectrophotometer.The metal ions were determined by inductively coupled plasma atomic emission spectrometer.The surface photovoltage (SPV) measurement was performed with a lock-in based SPV measurement system at room temperature and in air atmosphere. The activity measurements for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O were carried out in a gas-closed circulation system 19.A Xe lamp (300 W) purchased from Perfect Light Co.Light with a 420 nm UV-cutoff filter was used as the visible-light source.The reaction products were analyzed by using an on-line gas chromatograph (GC-9560; HuaAiSePu Corp., China).The photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 with H 2 O was typically carried out at 298 K for 480 min in each run and samples were continually taken from the reaction cell at given intervals (40 min) for quantitative analysis based on the external standard by using a calibration curve.
